items.original_suggestion: Validate the proposed workflow design with the client by walking the prototype through scripted demo scenarios that exercise each control (CFO threshold, dual auth, anomaly flag, escalation, CoP failure paths), and produce a clickable prototype the client can use to converge on the production specification.
High — comfortable across every screen and configuration setting
Demo integrity: ability to drive any scenario from a single login; mid stakes — the Super User exists to support demos rather than represent a real production role.
Daily during the POC build/demo phase.
Single-login access to every screen and action; ability to walk a demo without re-authenticating.
Being unable to exercise a control mid-demo because of a missing permission.
Mid — familiar with payment forms, beneficiaries, ERP exports; not deeply familiar with bank protocols.
High — a mistyped account number or sort code routes funds to the wrong recipient.
Multiple times a day; primary screen for this user.
Fast, low-friction capture; clear validation feedback before submit; clear status of what they have submitted.
Submitting a duplicate; entering wrong beneficiary details that pass validation but route incorrectly.
Mid-high — understands the duplicate, verification, and out-of-range signals on a payment.
High — wrongful approval results in funds leaving the business; controls compliance posture.
Several times a day in batches.
At-a-glance visibility of out-of-range and duplicate flags; bulk approve where safe.
Approving a payment that breaches the CFO threshold or that has a failing verification.
Mid-high — sees only payments escalated past SLA.
High — same as Approver 1 with the additional pressure of overdue items.
Daily during POC, sporadic outside SLA-breach windows.
Clear view of overdue items and how they got escalated.
Missing a payment that has aged past secondary-approval SLA.
High — domain expertise; uses the system selectively for high-value sign-off.
Very high — all payments above the CFO threshold (£500,000) and all anomalies (£1,000,000+) require their personal approval.
Daily but in short, focused sessions.
Filtered view of high-value items only; clear at-a-glance attestation that the verification, duplicate, and out-of-range checks have run.
Approving a £1M+ payment without seeing the anomaly indicator; missing a foreign payment that needs dual auth.
High — owns thresholds and toggles.
High — wrong threshold values misclassify CFO routing or anomaly flagging system-wide.
Rarely — set-and-forget; revisit when policy changes.
Clear units (currency vs minutes), validated input ranges, audit of who changed what.
Accidentally setting an SLA in the wrong unit (hours vs minutes) or dropping a threshold by an order of magnitude.
Mid — knows the bank-rail differences (Faster, BACS, CHAPS, SWIFT) at a working level.
High — once released, a payment cannot be undone.
Several times a day in release windows.
Confidence in the batch contents before transmission; single confirmation step; quick batch export for audit.
Releasing the wrong payment; missing a partial-match receipt during reconciliation.
Time-to-submit; zero data-entry errors at submit; clear in-line validation feedback.
Visibility of inbound queue; one-click submit-to-workflow per row.
Verification result visible before activation; duplicate prevention.
Bulk action efficiency; visibility of out-of-range / duplicate / verification flags; SLA awareness.
Pre-release confirmation; post-release attestation showing batch contents.
Match status visibility (Settled / Partial / Unmatched); one-screen view.
Single-screen authoritative source; clear units; change auditability.
Filterable history; CSV/PDF download.
At-a-glance KPIs; period filter affects all widgets; click-through to detail screens.
One-click reset; predictable post-reset data set.
Clear "overdue" filter and visual indicator.
Confirmation modal that names the amount and beneficiary.
Surface a navigation menu where every screen is reachable in one click.
Numeric inputs validated against min/max bands.
Any persona with audit-read may download; default include Release Officer + CFO + Configurator + Super User.
Super User only (and any persona with reset permission — POC limited to Super User).
Mobile breakpoint table-to-card collapse per [STANDARD-RULE: GR-18] (below 768 px). Note: dashboard and approval queues are desktop-primary; mobile is best-effort.

items.source_location: §6.5 RBAC · Release Officer · Reset demo cell
items.original_suggestion: X (Release Officer may execute Reset demo).

Cell-level inferences across the matrix not directly stated in inputs (Approver tiers' read access to Beneficiaries; Release Officer's update right on Bank Receipts; CFO's broad read across operational entities) — re-confirm the matrix as a whole.
Prototype handles no real PAN data; payment instrument fields are bank account numbers (UK Sort/Account, IBAN/SWIFT) and not card primary account numbers.

items.source_location: §6.6.4 C-03 UK FCA segregation of duties
items.original_suggestion: UK FCA-aligned segregation of duties: distinct Initiator / Approver / Release Officer roles; CFO sign-off above £500k; dual-auth on Foreign — already encoded in §6.2 BR-01 / BR-03.

GDPR / UK-GDPR: Beneficiary contact email and personal-name data is stored. PII redaction on screen / consent banner — visual manifestation: redact PII columns for personas without read on Beneficiary.
WCAG 2.2 AA target across all screens (colour-pairing per UF-04, keyboard nav, focus management per GR-07, aria labelling per GR-17).
Screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), keyboard-only operation, high-contrast mode.
DD/MM/YYYY; not in past beyond business days
ReasonCode { Supplier invoice, Refund, Payroll, Adhoc }
~10²–10³ active beneficiaries; ~10³–10⁴ payments per quarter; receipts ≈ 1× payments.
~10–10² payments captured per business day during demo; ERP feed simulated at 1–10 payments per batch.
5–10 concurrent users during demo windows; production assumption ~50–100 concurrent users for medium UK enterprise treasury.
